expectations.text: A canonical v3 HTML file exists at the top level of the wireframes folder (not inside _historical/). Filename suggests it's the v3/canonical version.
A _historical/ subfolder exists at the wireframes folder root and contains the superseded v1 and v2 HTMLs.
expectations.evidence: Used 'archive/' not '_historical/'. The skill's prescribed quarantine naming was not followed; any reader expecting _historical/ won't find it. This is exactly the failure mode the skill prevents (ambiguity between canonical and superseded artifact naming).

expectations.text: A README.md exists at the wireframes folder root that functions as a design-intent spec.
expectations.evidence: README.md exists with sections: Selected, Iteration history, Recovery note, Process gap surfaced

expectations.text: The README has a section titled 'Locked design rules' or similar.
expectations.evidence: No section names locked rules. README focuses on iteration history + recovery, not on what's immutable about the design.

expectations.text: The README has a section listing/indexing the canonical files.
expectations.evidence: No explicit 'Canonical files index' section. 'Selected: v3' implies it but doesn't list/index all files at the wireframes root.

expectations.text: The README has a designer-handoff section distinguishing Locked vs Open for craft input.
expectations.evidence: No designer handoff section. README is internal-facing (notes on the process gap, recovery), not designer-facing.

expectations.text: Each canonical HTML file contains an HTML comment near the top documenting what the file represents (mode/state/version).
expectations.evidence: v3 HTML header: 'PLACEHOLDER — admin-dashboard-v3 (SELECTED)' with design intent block. Clear about what it represents and that visuals were lost.

expectations.text: The wireframes folder is located under outputs/systems/admin-console/reference/wireframes/ matching the skill's location pattern.
